text: The University of Central Punjab's vision is to become an internationally acclaimed university in teaching and research. Its mission is to provide quality education leading to research, employability and entrepreneurial pursuits, creating societal impact, serving the nation and the world.

UCP's core values emphasize integrity, respect, tolerance, commitment, service and creativity; the university highlights honesty, ethical responsibility, dignity, acceptance of different views, passion to work hard, helping the community, and encouraging innovation.

In his message, Chairman Mian Amer Mahmood describes UCP as a nationally recognized university for academic and extracurricular achievements. He notes the diverse student body from across Pakistan and emphasises UCP's role in developing young minds through academics, social and athletic activities. He encourages students to take advantage of world-class resources and faculty and explains that UCP promotes multidisciplinary research and encourages students to become job creators.

Pro‑Rector Dr. Hammad Naveed welcomes students and parents, describing UCP as a transformational journey that equips students with skills to thrive in an ever‑changing world. He emphasises outstanding teaching, scholarships, flexible programs and extracurricular opportunities. He recounts that Punjab Group of Colleges petitioned the Government of Punjab in 1996, obtained a No Objection Certificate in 1999, and that UCP's charter was granted in April 2002, making it the first private sector university in Punjab to become a degree-awarding institution. The pro‑rector highlights UCP’s research‑driven culture, high‑tech facilities, solar‑powered campus and numerous societies promoting education, socialization, entrepreneurship and the arts.

UCP invites applications twice a year (Fall and Spring). Applicants should research programmes on the university website, complete the online application form, pay the processing fee online or at the Admissions Office, submit the form through the online portal, and then receive a reference number along with test and interview dates. Merit lists are published on the official website and communicated to candidates by email and SMS. Successful candidates must deposit dues by the deadline to receive registration numbers and course details, and all students attend an orientation session.

The Student Services Center (SSC) provides a one-stop service hub for student queries. It aims to offer quality academic and non‑academic services under one roof through a trained team using modern IT tools. The SSC acts as the main point of contact for students during their stay at UCP, providing a friendly and supportive environment, bridging students and administration, and offering support services to help students enjoy campus life.

The Quality Enhancement Cell (QEC) at UCP focuses on planning, guiding and monitoring quality assurance and enhancement. Established in line with the Higher Education Commission (HEC) quality assurance framework, the QEC works to maintain and enhance the quality of education and research at the University of Central Punjab.

The Career Services Office (CSO) prepares students for life after university by helping them explore employment and further education opportunities. It has three key verticals — placements, continuing education and alumni relations — and promotes mentorship and networking among students and alumni. The CSO seeks to enhance the UCP experience beyond campus by developing a strong alumni network to increase capacity and knowledge sharing.

The Office of Research, Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) at UCP is established according to HEC requirements to develop and enhance research-based innovation and commercialization activities. ORIC ensures that research programs comply with directives from recognizing authorities. It operates a Directorate of Research to strengthen research efforts, an Industrial Linkages section to build partnerships between the university and the corporate sector for applied research and technology transfer, and Takhleeq — a business incubator that nurtures entrepreneurs through events, lectures and counseling.

In his message, the Dean of the Faculty of Information Technology and Computer Science (FoIT & CS) states that the faculty offers a wide range of degree programs in computing and provides high-end technology resources. The faculty aims to train students to cope with the ever-evolving discipline of computing and to become technology entrepreneurs and leaders. The faculty houses departments of Computer Science and Software Engineering and offers bachelor’s, master’s and PhD programs in computer science, software engineering and data science.

Established in 2002, the Faculty of Information Technology and Computer Science has world-class faculty and research centres such as the R&D Centre, Game Centre, Medical Informatics Centre, Learning Design Centre and Robotics Centre. Its curriculum is aligned with high-tech industry trends and offers balanced theoretical and practical courses. The faculty’s research centres conduct work in areas like machine learning, data analytics, healthcare, robotics, embedded systems, mathematics, information retrieval and the Internet of Things. Facilities include teaching, research and general laboratories with more than 1,700 computers, campus-wide connectivity and a dedicated hardware lab. The faculty’s infrastructure supports students seeking careers as data scientists, analysts, technicians, cybersecurity specialists and software developers.

The Centre for Learning and Design at UCP aims to make education effective, accessible and affordable. Initiatives include learning management systems (LMS) for the Faculty of IT and UCP Business School, a hypermedia learning system for English, a zero-semester program for computing and mathematics, and a classroom response system for automatic quizzes.

The Centre for Game Design supports students who wish to specialise in game design, particularly for educational technology. It helps diversify Pakistan’s IT industry by building linkages between the game industry, computer scientists and instructional designers.
The Centre for Mathematics Teaching and Research addresses poor learning achievements in mathematics by creating a platform to improve mathematics education and research.

The Centre for Robotics gives students hands‑on experience by allowing them to work with land‑based and aerial robots, building machines that incorporate artificial intelligence. The centre teaches design, modelling and control methods to prepare students for industry.

The Centre for Healthcare Modeling and Informatics conducts state‑of‑the‑art research in health informatics, bioinformatics, biostatistics, machine learning, computer vision and software engineering to develop intelligent solutions that support evidence‑based healthcare.

UCP’s facilities for media and communication studies include a state‑of‑the‑art production house equipped with modern media production facilities, providing students with experience in television and cinema production. The university operates FM 92.6 ‘UCP Ki Dunya,’ a 24/7 radio station offering infotainment, educational programs, talk shows and debates to train future radio professionals. UCP’s non‑linear editing labs house 40 specialized computers installed with the latest applications to support video editing and 2D animation projects for media students.

The Environmental Protection Society at UCP trains students to protect the environment by promoting resource efficiency, energy conservation and waste minimization. It mobilizes resources to develop an environmentally friendly, energy‑efficient and waste‑minimising society.
UCP offers multiple types of scholarships including merit‑based, financial aid, need‑based and extracurricular scholarships. The scholarship policy effective from Fall 2025 applies to new admissions.

The fee structure for 2025‑26 lists per‑credit‑hour fees and admission fees for various programs. For example, the Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) program in the Faculty of Management Sciences has a per‑credit‑hour fee of 13,810 Pakistani rupees and an admission fee of 25,000 rupees. Scholarship options reduce the first‑semester installment; with a 25% scholarship the first installment is 128,575 rupees and with a 50% scholarship it is 97,503 rupees.

The University of Central Punjab is a private university in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan. It was established in 2002 and its motto is ‘The centre of your future’. The university has over 602 academic staff and more than 16,000 students, including around 14,400 undergraduates and 1,600 postgraduates. The main campus is located at 1 Khayaban‑e‑Jinnah Road, Johar Town, Lahore.

UCP was petitioned for establishment in 1996 by the Punjab Group of Colleges, received a No Objection Certificate in 1999 and was chartered by the Government of Punjab in 2002. The campus, built in 2010, covers 500,000 square feet with five blocks and includes facilities such as an auditorium with 300 seats.

UCP comprises faculties including Engineering, Humanities and Social Sciences, Pharmaceutical Sciences, Law, Media and Communication Studies, Life Sciences, Information Technology and Computer Science, Language & Literature, Science & Technology and Management Sciences.

UCP’s engineering programs are accredited by the Pakistan Engineering Council under Level II (Washington Accord). The Pharm‑D program is accredited by the Pharmacy Council of Pakistan, and the Computer Science program is accredited by the National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC).

In the QS Asian University Rankings – Southern Asia 2025, the University of Central Punjab was ranked equal 94th. It was placed within the 801–1000 band in the Times Higher Education (THE) World University Rankings 2025 and equal 362nd in QS Asia 2025. UCP was also ranked 1201+ in the QS Sustainability Ranking 2025.

The Faculty of Management Sciences (FOMS) aims to provide education aligned with market requirements through case studies, projects, internships and ties with the corporate sector. Its dean states that FOMS strives to meet international standards by restructuring BBA and MBA programs, offering PhD and generalized MS programs, and providing experiential learning opportunities that develop proactive business leaders with a strong knowledge base and entrepreneurial mindset【269569453132670†L98-L119】.

FOMS traces its roots to the Punjab College of Business Administration (established 1991) and now has 23 PhD faculty members, state‑of‑the‑art facilities and international links with British and American universities. Its vision is to be a thought leader in business education at the intersection of globalisation and localisation, while the mission nurtures individuals, promotes transformational learning and contributes to society through morality, fairness, social responsibility and professionalism【269569453132670†L146-L163】.

In her message, the dean of the Faculty of Law highlights a three‑decade legacy rooted in Punjab Law College and notes that the faculty aims to produce “true human beings” motivated by qualities of head and heart. The program blends regular law teaching with co‑curricular activities and practical exposure; students are encouraged to go beyond course books and develop professionalism, acute observation and social awareness【499244537545578†L96-L112】.

The Faculty of Law emphasises experiential learning: students must complete pro‑bono service hours as part of their degree. The law clinic provides pro bono legal services to underprivileged communities and real‑life training for students, with facilities including a clinic office, secretary office and conference room【499244537545578†L140-L166】. The faculty also houses a Peace and Mediation Centre that promotes peace studies and mediation through academic courses and practical research【499244537545578†L172-L189】.

The faculty’s overview notes that it evolved from Punjab Law College (established 1987) and became part of UCP in 2016. It focuses on developing three attributes in law students: critical observation and problem‑solving skills; ethical credence to resolve dilemmas; and social empathy. Education at FOL applies “law in the book” as “law in action” and offers varied opportunities to help students become productive members of society【499244537545578†L134-L145】.

The Faculty of Languages and Literature (FLL), formed after UCP’s 2022 act split the former Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences, houses departments of English Literature and Linguistics along with English, Chinese and Japanese language centres. The faculty continues to offer BS English Language and Literature and MS English Literature programmes and adds MS Applied Linguistics and PhD Linguistics; language centres provide courses and support across the university【688218767409298†L97-L114】.

FLL’s mission is to provide an interactive and robust learning environment with highly qualified faculty (eight PhDs) and a contemporary curriculum that encourages critical inquiry and independent thinking. Students gain well‑developed communication and analytical skills and can pursue careers in publishing, advertising, public relations, research, technical writing and education【688218767409298†L116-L129】.

The BS English Language and Literature is a four‑year programme combining literature and linguistics courses to prepare graduates for careers in media, business, diplomacy and further studies. The MS English Literature is a two‑year programme covering core literature and theory; applicants need at least 2.0/4.0 CGPA in a BS English degree or 50 % marks in MA English and must pass the UCP admission test and interview【688218767409298†L155-L170】.

The Department of Linguistics offers MS Applied Linguistics (two‑year) and PhD Linguistics programmes focusing on language teaching and theoretical/applied linguistics. Applicants for the MS programme require at least 2.0/4.0 CGPA in BS English or 50 % marks in a relevant master’s degree, while PhD applicants need a 3.0/4.0 CGPA in MS/M.Phil Linguistics or ELT and must pass the UCP admission test and interview【688218767409298†L173-L200】.

UCP’s English Language Centre (ELC) integrates teaching and research, viewing high proficiency in English as critical for student success across disciplines. Courses have been revamped to meet global standards and are taught in audiovisual‑equipped classrooms where students practise all four language skills. ELC encourages teachers to conduct classroom action research to diagnose students’ needs and adapt materials accordingly【688218767409298†L205-L218】.

The Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences (FOHSS) offers a scholastically rich and culturally diverse environment with disciplines including Political Science, International Relations, Economics, Psychology and Physical Education & Sports Sciences. Its dean emphasises a unique pedagogy that blends traditional and innovative methods to develop critical thinking and encourages students to engage with scholars and professionals through societies such as the Voltaire Society, Young Economists’ Society and Psychology Society; the faculty aims to groom future civil servants, journalists, analysts, researchers, economists and psychologists【12649750759835†L99-L115】.

FOHSS is a dynamic academic entity with 14 PhD faculty members and a strong research focus. The faculty comprises departments of Psychology, Political Science & International Relations and Economics, covering undergraduate to postgraduate levels. Its programmes embed employability themes and encourage knowledge transfer and consultancy with public and private enterprises【12649750759835†L123-L137】.

The Department of Psychology recognises the growing role of psychology in business, management and social sciences. It was established to cater to students pursuing careers in academia or industry, and plans to launch training programmes for professionals in business and management. The department also offers introductory courses to students from other faculties and aims to standardise its teaching input【12649750759835†L142-L156】.

The Department of Political Science & International Relations offers BS programmes in International Relations and Political Science. It seeks to attract bright scholars with highly qualified faculty and provides a stimulating academic environment. Its goal is to produce graduates with deep understanding of institutions, practices and relations that constitute public life and to develop modes of inquiry that promote citizenship and success in a global world【12649750759835†L160-L173】.

The Department of Economics emphasises the critical role of economists in socio‑economic growth and aims to train students with cutting‑edge knowledge. Initially supporting BBA and MBA programmes, it launched the BS Economics and Finance programme in 2011 and later added specialisations in Finance and Data Analytics. The MS programme began in 2013; graduates work in public and private sectors and have research evaluated by experts【12649750759835†L177-L203】.

The dean of the Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences notes that UCP, recognised by the Pharmacy Council of Pakistan and HEC since 2011, provides high‑standard professional education in a conducive environment. The faculty hosts world‑class educators and researchers exploring new frontiers in drug discovery and translational clinical research, producing more than 30 manuscripts over three years. The message emphasises innovation, learning, research, professionalism and diversity【309071364365166†L95-L116】.

Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences (FOP), established in 2011, prepares students to serve in various pharmacy sectors including industry, hospitals, community pharmacies, forensic science and regulatory bodies like DRAP. All programmes are accredited by PCP and HEC, and FOP has 10 PhD faculty members. Its learner‑centred approach emphasises critical thinking, professionalism, life‑long learning, community service and leadership【309071364365166†L126-L140】.

Salient features of the Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences include accreditation with the Pakistan Pharmacy Council and HEC; membership in the International Pharmaceutical Federation’s Academic Institution program (FIP‑AIM); internationally trained faculty; advanced teaching, learning and assessment methodologies; well‑equipped laboratories; advanced research facilities for all levels; national and international research collaborations; a friendly multicultural environment; and strong partnerships with the pharmaceutical industry and hospitals【309071364365166†L142-L156】.

The dean of the Faculty of Information Technology and Computer Science emphasises that the faculty offers a wide range of degree programs supported by high‑end computers and state‑of‑the‑art technology. Faculty and staff aim to produce technology entrepreneurs and highly skilled leaders capable of adapting to ever‑evolving computing disciplines. The faculty has two departments—Computer Science and Software Engineering—and offers bachelor’s, master’s and PhD programmes in Computer Science, Software Engineering and Data Science【922280929294397†L92-L106】.

The department traces its history to the Punjab Institute of Computer Science established in 1993; its programmes improved higher education quality in partnership with other institutions and produced graduates who became technology entrepreneurs and held top positions in IT companies. New programmes in Software Engineering and Data Science provide students with more choices in the information technology field【922280929294397†L108-L116】.

Established in 2002, the Faculty of Information Technology and Computer Science has 13 PhD faculty members and aims to make Pakistan a leader in computer science and software development. Its Research and Development Centre, Game Centre, Centre for Medical Informatics, Centre for Learning & Design and Robotics Centre complement a curriculum aligned with high‑tech industry trends, offering balanced theoretical and practical courses and preparing students for careers as data scientists, analysts, cybersecurity specialists and developers【922280929294397†L135-L152】.

FOIT has launched six research centres that conduct cutting‑edge research in machine learning, data analytics, healthcare, robotics, embedded systems, mathematics, information retrieval and IoT. The Centre for Learning & Design develops educational tools such as learning management systems and hypermedia systems; the Centre for Game Design supports game development and EdTech; the Council for Mathematics Teaching & Research addresses challenges in mathematics education; the Centre for Robotics offers hands‑on robotics experience and collaborations with German labs; and the Centre for Healthcare Modeling & Informatics applies informatics, machine learning and computer vision to public health【922280929294397†L154-L217】.

To support students, FOIT maintains eight teaching laboratories, four research labs and three general labs equipped with over 1,700 computers. The faculty operates its own ISP, virtual labs and campus‑wide network connectivity managed by qualified technical staff to provide students with state‑of‑the‑art computing resources【922280929294397†L219-L229】.

The Faculty of Engineering (FOE) includes three departments—Civil, Electrical and Mechanical—and aims to produce efficient engineers with strong problem‑solving skills, technical expertise and ethical values. The dean’s message highlights the faculty’s focus on outcome‑based education, updated laboratories, modern workshops and resources, and research‑led teaching that fosters curiosity, leadership and teamwork【440407358497083†L95-L120】.

Established in 2002, the Faculty of Engineering combines world‑class faculty (over 20 PhDs and 50 master’s degree holders) with a research‑focused curriculum. The faculty pursues international accreditation (Washington Accord) and encourages students to innovate, lead and serve society through curiosity and long‑term professional growth【440407358497083†L124-L142】.

The B.Sc. Electrical Engineering programme aims to develop graduates who can solve complex industrial problems and lead responsibly. Started in fall 2003, all graduating batches are accredited by the Pakistan Engineering Council; since the fall 2021 and fall 2022 cohorts the programme has achieved Level‑II OBE accreditation, making its graduates eligible for recognition by Washington Accord signatories. The department operates 13 laboratories and established a Space Research Centre in 2020 to promote space technology education【412273342416922†L93-L115】.

The BBA programme provides a broad understanding of business processes with majors in management, finance, marketing, information and operations management, and entrepreneurship. It emphasises communication skills and exposure to social sciences and equips students with planning, organizing, team‑building and problem‑solving skills; knowledge of social, economic and political realities; a holistic management perspective; policy formulation abilities; ethical awareness; interpersonal skills; and quantitative and qualitative tools for entrepreneurship【757334598642699†L70-L112】. The 132‑credit‑hour curriculum includes core, compulsory, general and elective courses along with a project or community service component【757334598642699†L117-L139】.

The Faculty of Media and Mass Communication (FMMC) aims to meet international standards by providing in‑depth knowledge, industry‑based skills, critical thinking and creativity in media and communication. Established in 2014 with over 1,000 students, FMMC offers BS, MSc, MPhil and PhD programmes covering journalism, film, advertising, digital media and research. Facilities include a TV studio, production house, non‑linear editing lab and FM 92.6 radio station, and the faculty maintains partnerships and internships with media organisations【183933715127548†L96-L137】【183933715127548†L145-L233】.

The Faculty of Science & Technology (FOST) introduces students to natural sciences across seven departments: Basic & Applied Chemistry, Microbiology, Food Science & Technology, Mathematics, Physics, Zoology/Botany and Biotechnology. The dean’s message emphasises practical and theory‑based learning with modern laboratories, encourages creativity and collaboration, and promotes foreign linkages that enhance research and student visits【100684965642621†L98-L126】. The overview notes that FOST has more than 54 PhD scholars and has produced over 200 research publications annually since 2016; it fosters interdisciplinary research and addresses modern challenges in physical, biological and food sciences【100684965642621†L132-L155】.

UCP’s media hub includes a production house equipped with new‑generation media production facilities, giving students hands‑on experience in television and cinema; the university also operates a radio station (FM 92.6) that provides 24‑hour infotainment and trains students as radio jockeys, producers and broadcasters; non‑linear editing labs with 40 specialised computers support video projects and 2D animation, making them some of the most advanced facilities in Pakistan【344325698880630†L79-L104】.

The campus sports complex promotes physical wellbeing with separate gyms for male and female members, a 25‑yard all‑weather swimming pool (four lanes, depth 3–6 feet), and recreational facilities like table tennis and foosball【344325698880630†L160-L165】. The cafeteria and food street offer two air‑conditioned cafeterias, diverse snacks and meal options, a food street with traditional snacks and barbecue, and a subsidised executive lounge for faculty and staff, maintaining high hygiene standards【344325698880630†L200-L210】.

The UCP library holds approximately 40,000 textbooks and 2,350 reference books (dictionaries, encyclopedias, directories, Islamic collections etc.) and provides access to research databases like IEEE, JSTOR, McGraw‑Hill, Project MUSE, Taylor & Francis and Wiley‑Blackwell. The library is open during university working hours and offers comfortable spaces for individual and group study【344325698880630†L293-L303】.

The university maintains state‑of‑the‑art laboratories for engineering, IT, pharmacy and science to support hands‑on learning and experimentation. It also offers a girls’ hostel and on‑campus transportation services (shuttle) to facilitate students’ living and commuting needs【344325698880630†L365-L370】.

UCP’s scholarship policy for Fall 2025 introduces fifteen categories of scholarships, including scholarships for Punjab Group of Colleges students, alumni, merit, need, specially‑abled students, talent, sports, legacy, Gilgit‑Baltistan students, educators, media and engineering professionals, post‑graduate fellowships and support for students who lose a parent or guardian.  The aim is to support deserving students and provide a holistic educational experience by recognizing merit, need and talent【56455572281006†L4-L26】.

Under the Scholarship for Punjab Group of Colleges students, UCP awards a 50 % tuition waiver to students entering faculties such as Engineering, IT & Computer Science, Management Sciences, Humanities & Social Sciences, Media & Mass Communication, Science & Technology and Languages & Literature, and a 25 % waiver for Law and Pharmacy students.  To retain the scholarship students must achieve a continuation CGPA of 3.0 (or 2.0–3.0 for a 25 % award) after the first year; the waiver applies only for the standard program duration and requires completing at least 24 credit hours across two consecutive semesters【56455572281006†L29-L112】.

The Alumni Scholarship encourages UCP graduates to pursue MBA, MS or M.Phil. degrees by offering a tuition waiver of 50 % for students with a previous CGPA of 3.5 or higher, 25 % for a CGPA between 3.0–3.49 and 12.5 % after the first semester for those with a CGPA between 2.5–2.99.  Recipients must maintain the required continuation CGPA, take at least nine credit hours and complete their degree within the standard duration (two years)【56455572281006†L115-L156】.

UCP’s merit‑based scholarships for undergraduate students offer up to a 100 % tuition waiver to the top five position holders in any intermediate board.  For Engineering, IT and Law, 80 % marks qualify for a 75 % scholarship (continuation CGPA ≥3.70), 75 % marks for 50 % (CGPA ≥3.50), 70 % marks for 25 % (CGPA ≥3.25) and lower marks for a 12.5 % waiver (CGPA ≥3.00).  Different thresholds apply for Pharmacy (85 %, 80 %, 75 % and 70 %) and for Management Sciences, Humanities & Social Sciences, Media, Science & Technology and Languages & Literature (80 %, 75 %, 70 % and 65 %).  Scholarships are provisional at admission, reviewed after two semesters, and only cover the standard duration【56455572281006†L190-L266】.

For MBA and MS/M.Phil. students UCP offers merit‑based scholarships up to 75 %: applicants with a 16‑year degree CGPA of 3.80 receive 75 % (continuation CGPA ≥3.90), a CGPA of 3.60 earns 50 % (continuation 3.75‑3.89), and a 25 % scholarship is available after the first semester for students with a CGPA of 3.50‑3.74.  Recipients must maintain the required CGPA with a minimum nine‑credit‑hour workload and finish within the two‑year program duration【56455572281006†L267-L305】.

UCP’s need‑based scholarship provides 25 % to 100 % financial aid for undergraduate students facing financial hardships.  Applicants must submit a form at the admission portal; awards cover the first year and require maintaining at least a 2.50 CGPA and completing 30 credit hours over two consecutive semesters.  The scholarship only applies during the standard program duration【56455572281006†L307-L349】.

Specially‑abled students may receive 25 % to 100 % tuition waivers at UCP (25–100 % for undergraduate programs and 25–75 % for graduate programs).  Applicants must provide a government‑certified disability certificate.  To continue the scholarship students must maintain a CGPA of at least 2.50 (or 2.25–2.50 for a reduced 25 % award) with minimum credit hours, and the waiver covers only the standard program duration【56455572281006†L351-L390】.

UCP awards Talent‑Based Scholarships to students with exceptional achievements in curricular and extracurricular activities.  The scholarship ranges from 25 % to 100 % of tuition and requires a minimum CGPA of 2.00 for continuation; it applies only during the normal duration of the degree program【56455572281006†L402-L428】.

Sports‑Based Scholarships support athletic students by providing full or partial tuition waivers.  Recipients must maintain a CGPA of at least 2.00 and the scholarship applies only for the standard duration of the degree program【56455572281006†L431-L457】.

The Legacy Scholarship grants a 25 % tuition waiver to siblings of current UCP students and children of UCP alumni.  Recipients must maintain a CGPA of 3.00 and the waiver covers only the normal duration of the degree program【56455572281006†L460-L479】.

Students holding a Gilgit‑Baltistan domicile receive a 30 % tuition fee waiver for all undergraduate and graduate programs at UCP.  Beneficiaries must maintain a minimum CGPA of 2.70; female students also receive free hostel accommodation (room charges) and the waiver only applies for the program’s standard duration【56455572281006†L482-L505】.

The Educator Advancement Program offers a flat 50 % scholarship to teachers employed in schools, colleges or universities who enrol in MS, M.Phil. or PhD programs at UCP.  Applicants need at least two years of professional teaching experience and must maintain a CGPA of 3.00 while remaining employed; the scholarship covers the standard program duration (two years for MS/M.Phil., three years for PhD)【56455572281006†L507-L532】.

UCP’s scholarship for media professionals provides a flat 50 % tuition waiver for individuals with at least two years of professional experience who enrol in MS or M.Phil. programs in media and mass communication.  Recipients must maintain a CGPA of 3.00 and remain employed; the scholarship is valid for the standard two‑year duration【56455572281006†L534-L556】.

Engineering professionals pursuing M.Sc. programs at UCP receive a 50 % tuition waiver if they have at least two years of professional experience in a relevant engineering field.  Continuation requires a CGPA of 3.00 and ongoing employment; the scholarship covers only the standard program duration【56455572281006†L560-L586】.

UCP’s Post‑Graduate Fellowship Program supports full‑time MS and PhD scholars in the Faculty of Information Technology & Computer Science.  Fellows serve as lab instructors or lecturers, receive a stipend of Rs 50,000 (MS) or Rs 60,000 (PhD) and enjoy a 100 % fee waiver for four semesters (MS) or six semesters (PhD).  Fellows must teach 9–12 contact hours or 6 credit hours and the fellowship covers only the standard program duration【56455572281006†L588-L614】.

The Scholarship for Students with Deceased Parent/Guardian provides a 50 % tuition waiver for two semesters to currently enrolled students who lose a parent or guardian serving as the primary earner.  Eligible students must demonstrate that family assets are below Rs 15 million and annual income is under Rs 1.2 million【56455572281006†L616-L639】.

Important notes: scholarships are awarded as tuition fee waivers only and do not cover admission fees or other charges; they apply only during the normal duration of the degree program and are not available for repeat or improvement courses.  Recipients must abide by university rules and disciplinary policies—scholarships can be modified by the Academic Council and may be revoked for rule violations【56455572281006†L640-L670】.

The Student Services Centre (SSC) serves as a one‑stop hub for addressing student queries and providing facilitation under one roof.  A specially trained team uses modern IT and task‑resolution tools so students do not have to visit multiple offices.  The SSC works closely with administrative and academic departments to offer academic and non‑academic guidance and aims to create a friendly, supportive environment for students【942025654878689†L74-L90】.

UCP’s Career Services Office prepares students and alumni for their post‑university journey by offering pathways to better employment, foreign education opportunities and a strong connection with the institution.  The office operates through three ‘verticals’: placements (linking students to jobs), continuing education and alumni relations—leveraging the alumni network to provide mentorship, guidance and placement support【568970423933095†L74-L86】.

The Quality Enhancement Cell (QEC) at UCP plans, guides and monitors quality assurance activities in accordance with the Higher Education Commission’s quality assurance framework.  Its mission is to maintain and enhance the quality of education and research across the university【276068899689211†L75-L79】.

UCP’s Office of Research, Innovation & Commercialization (ORIC) acts as a hub for research excellence, innovation and commercialization.  ORIC aims to advance a culture of research excellence, bridge academia and industry, support high‑quality research, promote commercialization of university innovations and build strategic partnerships for national development.  Its vision is to be a catalyst for transformative research and innovation, and its mission emphasises research excellence, intellectual property generation and sustainable commercialization【699697662927567†L50-L75】.

UCP invites applications twice a year (Fall and Spring; Engineering, Pharmacy and Law admit only in Fall).  Prospective students should explore programs, then complete the online application form and pay the processing fee.  After submission the applicant receives a reference number with admission test and interview details; merit lists are displayed online.  Successful candidates must deposit dues to receive their registration number and attend an orientation session【422040069300560†L76-L104】.

The Environmental Protection Society (EPS‑UCP) engages students in protecting the human and natural environment by using university resources sustainably.  Its goal is to train students to mobilize progress and nature for a modern, environmentally friendly, energy‑efficient and waste‑minimizing society【765869923588213†L124-L130】.

The International Crisis Chamber (ICC‑UCP) promotes the ideals of the United Nations—understanding, tolerance and acceptance—through debates and diplomacy.  It aims to cultivate a pluralistic mindset among students by simulating crisis diplomacy exercises, fostering social responsibility and preparing future leaders with a global perspective【765869923588213†L194-L205】.

The Islamic Guidance Club provides students with a circle to rejuvenate their Islamic spirit and broaden their vision. The society promotes equality, opposes bias and strives to nurture a well‑managed artistic community that continues to promote arts and crafts【608598060468674†L314-L323】.

The UCP Adventure Club offers students opportunities to explore different regions of Pakistan, from coasts to mountains, promoting tourism and fostering a positive national image. Its goal is to provide adventurous experiences and encourage students to discover the country’s natural beauty【608598060468674†L338-L344】.

The UCP Photography Club, initiated in 2011, creates an inclusive space for photographers. It has grown rapidly and organises workshops, photo walks, competitions, mega events and interactive sessions to help students practise their craft and develop their photographic skills【608598060468674†L362-L368】.

UCP hosted the International Conference on ‘Mental Health, Human Dignity and Climate Change: Mitigating Global Psychological Risks’ in 2025. Organised by the Department of Psychology, the event explored the interplay between human dignity, mental health and climate change, bringing together international scholars and mental health professionals to discuss how social inequality, environmental degradation and humiliation cycles affect psychological well‑being. Through keynote speeches, scientific sessions and panel discussions, the conference promoted cross‑cultural collaboration and collective action for global psychological sustainability【641154622677106†L79-L89】.

The Career Services Office organised a session titled ‘Mental Health & Nutrition’ for Faculty of Science & Technology students. Guest speaker Dr. Sharmeen Yaseen explained how balanced nutrition supports mental health, academic performance and personal growth, encouraging students to adopt healthy lifestyles and better plan their futures【721788623061709†L75-L80】.

In its sustainability efforts, UCP emphasises global welfare and aligns with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Key areas include academic programmes, research initiatives, industry collaboration and community engagement. For Good Health & Well‑being, UCP maintains a smoke‑free, drug‑free campus, runs health awareness programmes, collaborates with health authorities, organises AIDS prevention and blood donation drives and provides accessible accommodation with ramps, wheelchairs and lifts. A mental health community camp was held at General Hospital to discuss social, economic and cultural determinants of health【438653772051491†L144-L159】. UCP also pursues Clean Water & Sanitation by installing reverse‑osmosis plants and banning sugary drinks【438653772051491†L203-L211】, and Affordable & Clean Energy by integrating solar panels that generate around 512 kWh and cover roughly 75 % of campus consumption【438653772051491†L214-L218】. The university promotes gender equality by supporting women entrepreneurs (e.g., Takhleeq x Startup Grind Lahore event with 45 female entrepreneurs) and by enforcing merit‑based admissions【438653772051491†L186-L200】.

The Student Services Center (SSC) at UCP acts as a one‑stop service arena for student queries and facilitation.  It provides quality academic and non‑academic services under one roof so students don’t have to run between different offices.  The SSC works with administrative and academic departments to provide specialist and general advice and aims to create a friendly, supportive environment that bridges students and the university’s administration【610926873674043†L74-L90】.  It encourages students to stay in touch for updates and lists contact details: 1 Khayaban‑e‑Jinnah Road, Johar Town, Lahore, phone +92‑42‑35880007 / +92‑80‑000‑827 (9 am–5 pm), fax +92‑42‑35954892 and email info@ucp.edu.pk【610926873674043†L84-L126】.

UCP’s FAQ page answers common admission and portal questions.  Candidates can view their test/interview letter after paying and verifying the Rs 1,500 application processing fee【40674904928099†L74-L88】.  When attaching degrees, follow the instructions linked on the portal【40674904928099†L79-L82】.  For O/A‑level results across multiple pages, attach the page with maximum major grades and preferably include the IBCC equivalence【40674904928099†L84-L89】.  Fee verification can take 2–3 working days when paid at Albaraka Bank; candidates can email a copy of the paid challan to admissions@ucp.edu.pk【40674904928099†L91-L95】.  If the tuition fee challan is missing, students should visit the SSC for document verification【40674904928099†L97-L101】.  If signup credentials aren’t received via email, check the spam folder or sign up using a Google account.  Applicants can only apply to one Masters programme because each masters is specialised.  URLs should be typed in the browser address bar instead of the search engine【40674904928099†L113-L115】.  UCP does not require printing the admission form.  Timetables appear on the student portal once classes are scheduled【40674904928099†L121-L124】.  Weekend programmes include the MBA Executive and M.Phil. Media & Communication; other programmes have weekday classes【40674904928099†L126-L129】.  UCP charges tuition fees per semester, payable in two instalments【40674904928099†L131-L134】.  A regular semester lasts about 32 weeks or four months【40674904928099†L135-L137】.

The academic calendar outlines key dates for Semester Fall 2025 and Summer 2025.  For Fall 2025, orientation for new students runs 24–26 September, registration/add–drop for ongoing students ends 26 September, classes commence 29 September, mid‑term exams are scheduled 24–29 November (week 9), the last date to withdraw courses is 5 December, classes end 23 January 2026 (week 17) and final exams take place 26 January–4 February 2026 with grade notification on 10 February 2026【238063628070229†L74-L102】.  Public holidays such as Allama Iqbal Day (9 Nov 2025), Quaid‑e‑Azam Day (25 Dec 2025) and Kashmir Day (5 Feb 2026) are observed.  For Summer 2025, classes begin 28 July, registration/add–drop for ongoing students and payment of dues close on 25 July; mid‑term exams occur 21–23 August and final exams 18–20 September【238063628070229†L113-L134】.  Results are submitted on 23 September with grade notification on 24 September, followed by a semester break 22–27 September and the start of Fall 2025 classes on 29 September【238063628070229†L135-L145】.

The Student Handbook explains UCP’s fee refund policy for new admissions.  Admission fees are non‑refundable.  Tuition fees are fully refundable if a refund request is submitted before or during the first week of classes; 50% tuition is refundable if the request is made in the second week; no refund is given if the request is made after the second week【955484050388539†L784-L804】.  If the university cancels an advertised programme, 100% of tuition and admission fees are refunded【955484050388539†L805-L808】.  Refund requests must be made on the prescribed Fee Refund Form available from the Admissions Office【955484050388539†L810-L814】.

UCP’s attendance policy requires students to attend at least 80% of classes and labs.  Students missing more than 20% of classes are withdrawn from the course, with no transfer of dues or tuition fee【955484050388539†L892-L899】.  Attendance is counted from the first day of classes; late registration counts as absence and may lead to insufficient attendance【955484050388539†L901-L908】.  Four absences are treated as leave and incur no fine, but fines may apply beyond that; after six absences the course is automatically withdrawn with a W grade on the transcript【955484050388539†L910-L918】.  Students delaying registration and fee payment are marked absent, which can lead to being barred from the final exam【955484050388539†L901-L909】.

UCP’s evaluation system gives instructors discretion over the weighting of assessments but provides a broad guideline: quizzes, assignments, projects and class participation collectively count for 30–40% of the grade; mid‑term examinations count for about 20%; and final examinations count for 40–50%【955484050388539†L936-L947】.  Mid‑term and final exams are administered by the Controller of Examinations Office, which circulates the date sheet.  Exams conducted without COE approval are not valid【955484050388539†L847-L854】.

UCP allows transfer of credits subject to strict conditions.  The transfer institution must be regionally accredited and HEC‑approved, and the course must match UCP’s in content and level.  Only courses with grades C+ or higher (undergraduate) or B‑ or higher (graduate) are eligible【955484050388539†L985-L1014】.  Credits accepted appear on the UCP transcript without grades and may not exceed 50% of the program’s total credit hours【955484050388539†L1015-L1023】.  Students expelled from other institutions are not considered for credit transfer【955484050388539†L1024-L1027】.

Final term examinations at UCP occur after 16 weeks of teaching.  They are usually three hours long and scheduled by the Controller of Examinations Office; only students who meet attendance requirements and have cleared their semester dues may sit for the exam【955484050388539†L1030-L1047】.  There is ordinarily no provision for retaking the final exam; exceptions require documentation and approval from the COE and a relevant committee【955484050388539†L1048-L1056】.  The summer semester is used for makeup or deficiency courses; a 3‑credit theory course or 1‑credit lab can be completed in eight weeks.  Students may take up to 8 credits in summer, and course drops are not allowed【955484050388539†L1059-L1083】.
source: Student Handbook – Final Exams & Summer Semester

text: UCP’s Rules & Regulations 2025 specify admission eligibility criteria.  Candidates for undergraduate programmes must have at least 45% marks (Intermediate/A‑Levels).  For Engineering and Pharmacy programmes the minimum is 60% and for BS Computer Science, Software Engineering and Data Sciences it is 50%.  For MS/MPhil admission, applicants need a 16‑year bachelor’s degree with a minimum 2.0/4.0 CGPA or 50% marks; PhD applicants need an 18‑year master’s degree with a 3.0/4.0 CGPA or 60% marks【785603408839980†L76-L87】.  Admissions are granted on merit after meeting eligibility criteria【785603408839980†L88-L89】.

The registration process at UCP requires first‑semester courses to be assigned by the Dean’s Office, while in subsequent semesters students register themselves with advisor approval.  Registration dates are announced in advance; students must register and pay dues during these dates.  The portal enforces prerequisites, and students may revise their registration before classes start.  A typical academic load is 15–18 credit hours; with departmental permission, undergraduates may take up to 21 credit hours【785603408839980†L114-L130】.

UCP provides various scholarships and sets continuation conditions.  Scholarships are initially awarded based on the applicant’s previous academic credentials and may include kinship, PGC, alumni, merit, sports, need‑based, talent and disabled‑person categories【785603408839980†L160-L186】.  To continue receiving a scholarship, students must meet conditions related to their CGPA and academic load; failure to comply leads to suspension of the scholarship for at least one semester【785603408839980†L162-L173】.

Students wishing to withdraw from a course must submit a written request to the Dean or Head of Department within the dates published by the Registrar’s Office.  If approved, a W grade appears on the transcript and fees for that course are not refunded or carried forward【785603408839980†L283-L289】.  Students seeking a semester leave or freezing must obtain written permission from the Dean’s Office during the first week of the semester; without permission, they will be dropped from the university roll and treated as inactive.  Absence for two consecutive semesters results in cancellation of admission and re‑admission requires paying the admission fee again【785603408839980†L291-L302】.
source: Rules & Regulations 2025 – Course Withdrawal & Semester Leave

text: UCP’s career placement cell provides comprehensive support to help students secure jobs.  The career services office offers proper career counselling to help students choose career paths based on their abilities and interests.  It bridges the gap between students and the job market by guiding them about market realities and matching them with appropriate roles【278886600316635†L135-L145】.  The alumni relations vertical builds connections between current students and alumni to provide mentorship and job opportunities【278886600316635†L149-L156】.  The office also prepares students for job interviews by developing communication and self‑presentation skills【278886600316635†L162-L170】.  Students can contact the career services office for guidance via email at cso@ucp.edu.pk【278886600316635†L182-L189】.

The Department of Student Affairs oversees the Student Council, which organizes and manages extra‑ and co‑curricular activities for the student body through various societies.  These societies are managed by students under a faculty patron and promote education, socialization, entrepreneurship, social causes and the arts.  Participation allows students to pursue their passions and develop skills outside the classroom while working with university management and student leaders【706421778713757†L74-L82】.

UCP’s Psychology Department promotes mental health awareness through events such as the 2017 Mental Health Camp.  The camp was organised by MS Clinical Psychology students under faculty supervision and offered tests for self‑compassion, self‑esteem, exam anxiety, social anxiety and personality assessment.  Professional counsellors provided on‑the‑spot counselling to students discussing personal issues.  Approximately 350 students registered for the counselling centre, which gave them an opportunity to discuss their issues with clinical psychologists【229733593231711†L110-L123】.

UCP adheres to the Higher Education Commission’s Policy on Protection Against Sexual Harassment in Higher Education Institutions.  The policy affirms the right of every member of the university community to study and work in an environment free from sexual harassment and makes such conduct a punishable offence.  Sexual harassment includes unwelcome sexual advances, requests for favours or other verbal, written or physical conduct of a sexual nature; it can involve overt or subtle behaviours, non‑consensual contact, sexual exploitation or other actions that create an intimidating, hostile or offensive environment【159946260104328†L50-L92】【159946260104328†L120-L159】.  The policy outlines the principles, prohibited conduct, jurisdiction, designated resources for reporting, investigation procedures, penalties and educational measures to prevent harassment【159946260104328†L47-L104】.

The Exam Office page provides downloadable forms and guidelines for students.  Forms available include the guideline for students, transcript issuance proforma (white), degree issuance proforma (white), proforma for change of name/father’s name or date of birth (yellow), thesis submission form, proforma for urgent degree issuance (red), proforma for rectification of transcript title (green) and a voucher for photograph【405236334384155†L74-L87】.  These resources help students handle exam‑related administrative tasks and can be downloaded from the Exam Office web page.

For problems related to courses that students have already enrolled in, the proper point of contact is the Registrar Office located in Building A.  Students should visit the Registrar Office to resolve issues such as course registration adjustments, enrollment errors, or academic record updates (user‑provided information).

Students looking for any building or location on campus should use the Campus Map feature available in the Ask‑UCP app.  The map helps you locate classrooms, offices, labs, hostels, cafeterias and other facilities (user‑provided information).

When submitting tuition fee or challan forms, students can choose from two bank locations on campus: one bank branch is inside the Admissions Office and another branch is near the Food Street (user‑provided information).  These branches accept fee deposits and issue receipts to students.

Volunteers in Service (VIS) is a mandatory requirement for degree completion at UCP.  Students must participate in the VIS program and complete the required number of service hours before they can graduate (user‑provided information).

Elevators are available in Buildings B and C for student use; however, Building A does not offer elevator access for students.  Students should use staircases in Building A and plan accordingly when moving between floors (user‑provided information).
To view your test or interview letter on the admission portal, you must pay the Rs 1500 admission application processing fee and wait for it to be verified.
If your degrees are not attaching in the academic section, follow the degree attachment process provided on the UCP website.
For O/A‑levels results that are spread over multiple pages, attach the page with the highest major grades and include your IBCC equivalence certificate.

If your admission application processing fee isn’t verified, automatic verification takes 2–3 working days when paid via Albaraka Bank; you can email a copy of the paid challan to admissions@ucp.edu.pk for assistance.
If your tuition fee challan isn’t uploaded on the portal, visit the Student Services Centre to verify documents and obtain your fee challan.
If signup credentials aren’t received (e.g., using a Yahoo ID), check your spam folder and consider signing up with a Google account.
There is no preference for Master’s programmes because each is a specialized degree; applicants may apply only to one programme relevant to their background.
To access the URL etest@ucp.edu.pk, paste it directly into your browser’s address bar rather than searching for it.
UCP does not require you to print and submit the admission form manually.
Students can view their timetable on the portal once classes are scheduled.
UCP offers weekend classes only for the MBA Executive and M.Phil. Media & Communication programmes; all other programmes are held on weekdays.
UCP charges tuition on a per‑semester basis and divides it into two instalments.
A regular semester at UCP typically lasts 32 weeks or four months.

Dr. Hammad Naveed serves as Pro-Rector at the University of Central Punjab. He earned a Ph.D. in Bioinformatics from the University of Illinois at Chicago, completed post-doctoral research at the CAS-MPG Partner Institute for Computational Biology in China and at King Abdullah University of Science and Technology in Saudi Arabia, served as a Research Assistant Professor at the Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago from 2014 to 2017, and led FAST Lahore to become a premier campus, strengthening industry collaborations and educational quality.

Dr. Faisal Mustafa is Provost and Dean of Business at the University of Central Punjab. He has an 18-year international career spanning roles as professor, data scientist, business-intelligence consultant and IT transformation director. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Huddersfield (UK) and focuses his research on technology entrepreneurship, business intelligence, semantic web services, and business simulation modeling. He champions multidisciplinary curricula and inclusive, participatory learning.

Dr. Fawad Baig currently holds the additional charge as Dean of the Faculty of Media and Mass Communication at UCP. He completed his Ph.D. in Media & Communication from Technische Universität Dresden with magna cum laude and earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Education, Lahore. He has presented research at international conferences, won the Naomi C. Turner Prize for best paper, authored over 17 publications, serves as Pakistan’s principal investigator in the Journalistic Role Performance project, and previously spent fourteen years as a producer at Pakistan Television before returning to academia.

Prof. Dr. Taimoor-ul-Hassan is a senior professor of media studies at UCP and a pioneer of media education in Pakistan. With over 35 years of teaching and administrative experience, he has authored a seminal book on Pakistani media and served as dean at Beaconhouse National University before joining UCP in 2019. He chairs curriculum committees, mentors doctoral scholars, and maintains professional links with media organizations.

Dr. Hadia Awan is dean of the Faculty of Law at UCP. A former civil judge and principal of Punjab Law College, she oversees UCP’s law programmes and teaches judicial training courses. She holds a Ph.D. in Peace & Conflict Studies (2019) and an LL.M in International Dispute Resolution (2007). Selected by the British Council and U.S. programs for law teaching and leadership, she has served as dean since 2017 and previously led Punjab Law College (2006-17).

Prof. Dr. Barkat Ali is a specialist in constitutional and administrative law. With over 25 years of teaching experience, his research focuses on public-interest litigation and constitutional mandates. He holds a Ph.D. from the International Islamic University Islamabad (2020) and an LL.M in constitutional law from University of the Punjab (2008). He progressed from lecturer to professor at Islamia University Bahawalpur, serving as department head.

Muhammad Aamer Kamran is an assistant professor of law at UCP with 16 years of teaching and legal experience. He coordinates programmes and examinations and focuses on corporate and criminal law. He holds an LL.M in commercial law from BPP Law School (2015) alongside multiple postgraduate diplomas and master’s degrees and has held lecturer roles at various law colleges.

Shaista Anwar serves as an assistant professor and the Faculty Industry Liaison Officer for UCP’s law faculty. She manages industry linkages, co-edits the UCP Journal of Law, and researches jurisprudence, international law, and legal theory. She holds an LLM in international law (2017) and an LL.B (2003) and has coordinated UCP’s LL.B programme and served on advisory committees.

Ammara Afzal Siddiqui is an assistant professor of law at UCP. A long-serving educator and examination coordinator, she encourages moot courts and debates. Her research covers tort law, gender and law, and legal English. She earned an LL.M in business law from Girne American University (2005) and an LL.B from University of the Punjab (2002) and has served at UCP since 2006 in teaching and administrative roles.

Freeha Khaled is an assistant professor of law specializing in international law. She holds an LL.M in international law (2019), an LL.B (2011), and commerce degrees. Since 2016 she has taught at UCP and previously worked as a visiting lecturer and legal associate. Her publications address reproductive rights, educational technology in the Muslim world, and human-rights compliance in conflict situations.

Prof. Dr. Mahtab Ahmad Khan is dean of UCP’s Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences. A pharmacology expert and translational neuroscience researcher, he emphasises outcome-based education, curriculum reform, and international collaborations. He earned a Ph.D. in Pharmacology from the University of Lübeck (2015), an M.Phil in Pharmacology (2006), and a B.Pharmacy (1998) and previously taught at UCP and University of Lahore.

Dr. Muhammad Amjad Iqbal is dean of UCP’s Faculty of Information Technology & Computer Science. An expert in machine learning, fuzzy logic, and data mining, he holds a Ph.D., M.Phil., and MSc in Computer Science. He has served as faculty member and researcher and has published work on fuzzy set theory, decision support systems, and data mining.

Dr. Mohsin Ashraf heads the Department of Computer Science at UCP. His expertise spans machine learning, computer vision, and data mining. He earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science (2021), an MS (2015), and a BS (2004). He has taught at UCP and the University of Lahore and researches deep-learning, object detection, and algorithm optimisation.

Dr. Abbas Khalid, professor and associate dean, specialises in communication systems, wireless networks, and the Internet of Things. He holds a Ph.D. in Communication Systems from Lancaster University (2012) and an MS in Computer Science from UCP (2005) and has worked at multiple universities and served on national committees. His publications examine multiple access techniques, power line communications, and IoT architectures.

Dr. Muhammad Rizwan Shad is dean of UCP’s Faculty of Engineering. A mechanical engineer with expertise in rotor dynamics and applied mechanics, he champions outcome-based education. He obtained a Ph.D. from INSA Toulouse, France (2011) and MSc and BSc degrees in Mechanical Engineering and has held roles as research scholar at INSA Toulouse and positions at SUPARCO. His research covers vibration analysis, rotor dynamics, and mechanical design.

Dr. Fesal Toosy is an associate professor and head of electrical engineering at UCP. Known for introducing robotics competitions and modern curricula, he holds a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UCP (2021), an MS from the University of Southern California (2001), and a BS from UET Lahore (1999). He has taught at UCP since 2003 and researches multichannel audio, robotics, and signal processing.

Dr. H. Rizwana Kausar serves as dean of UCP’s Faculty of Science & Technology. An applied mathematician and cosmologist, she is known for her work on general relativity and cosmology and for establishing research centres at UCP. She completed a post-doctoral fellowship at the University of Zurich, holds a Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics, and earned M.Phil. and MSc degrees from the University of the Punjab. She has decades of teaching experience and has published widely on gravitational physics and cosmological models.

Dr. Shazia Hasan heads the Department of Psychology at UCP. A clinical psychologist, she established the MS and PhD programmes and directs training clinics and research labs. She earned a Ph.D. in Clinical Psychology (University of Karachi, 2002), an M.Phil. and post-magisterial diploma, and leads research in psychotherapy, mental health, and psychometrics while supervising graduate research.

Prof. Dr. Khalid Manzoor Butt is dean of UCP’s Faculty of Languages & Literature (Humanities). A political scientist and academic leader, he previously served at GC University and researches politics, history, and media. He holds a Ph.D. in Political Science (2010) and earlier degrees from the University of the Punjab and has acted as chairperson and campus director. He has authored books and articles on South Asian politics and governance.

Dr. Tahir Saleem heads the Department of Linguistics at UCP. A linguist specialising in phonetics, sociolinguistics, and translation, he joined UCP as professor in 2024 and became department head in 2025. He holds a Ph.D. in Linguistics (2020), an MS in Applied Linguistics (2014), and an MA in English (2007) and researches phonetic variation, sociophonetics, and second-language acquisition.

The Student Services Center (SSC) serves as the primary point of contact for students during their time at UCP. It provides academic and non‑academic services under one roof, so students don’t have to visit multiple offices. SSC staff offer general advice and specialist support and act as a bridge between students and the university’s administration. The centre aims to create a friendly and supportive environment and to help students enjoy campus life by meeting the challenges they may face during their studies【609886321011454†L74-L90】.

UCP supports a vibrant extracurricular scene through numerous societies and clubs. These organizations range from innovation and entrepreneurship, welfare, environmental protection and gaming clubs to cultural and heritage societies, Islamic guidance, and international diplomacy. There are also special‑interest groups for automobiles (Motorhead Society), pets (Paws & Claws), fine arts (Takhleeqkar), adventure tourism, photography and many more. Each society provides opportunities for students to develop skills, cultivate friendships and give back to the community【622739267321679†L190-L206】.

UCP operates a Chinese Language Lab to support students preparing for the China–Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC). The university initiated Chinese language courses in collaboration with the Pak‑China Chamber of Commerce & Industry and Chinese universities. The Chinese Consulate General donated a well‑equipped lab that offers Level‑I and Level‑II Chinese language certification according to the Hanyu Shuiping Kaoshi (HSK) standards【835029197067688†L131-L139】.

The Media Hub within the Faculty of Media and Communication Studies houses a state‑of‑the‑art production house, a university radio station and non‑linear editing labs. The production house is equipped with modern technology to give students hands‑on experience in television and cinema production【835029197067688†L79-L83】. The FM radio station (FM 92.6 UCP Ki Dunya) airs 24/7 educational and infotainment programmes and trains students as radio jockeys, producers and anchors【835029197067688†L85-L95】. The non‑linear editing lab has forty specialized computers with the latest software for film editing, animation, graphic design and visual communication projects【835029197067688†L97-L104】.

UCP’s campus includes two air‑conditioned cafeterias, a lively food street and an executive lounge for faculty. The cafeterias provide snacks and meals throughout the day, while the food street offers canteen meals, barbecue and traditional snacks in a casual environment. Prices are subsidized to ensure affordability and the university strives to maintain high hygiene standards【835029197067688†L200-L210】.

UCP runs a fleet of twelve comfortable buses that follow eight routes across Lahore every hour. The transport office provides safe and affordable transport for students and faculty at nominal rates【835029197067688†L231-L237】.

The purpose‑built auditorium at UCP acts as the campus nerve centre for events. It is equipped with advanced lighting and sound systems and seats approximately 400 people. The auditorium hosts conferences, speaker sessions, musical and theatrical performances and national television recordings, adhering to international standards【835029197067688†L269-L277】.

UCP’s sports complex promotes physical well‑being with separate gymnasiums for male and female students. It includes a 25‑yard, all‑weather swimming pool with four lanes (3–6 ft deep) and provides recreational facilities such as table tennis and foosball【835029197067688†L160-L165】.

The UCP library complements classroom teaching by providing around 40,000 textbooks and 2,350 reference books, including dictionaries, encyclopedias and Islamic collections. It offers electronic access to databases such as IEEE, JSTOR, McGraw Hill, Project MUSE, Taylor & Francis and Wiley‑Blackwell journals, along with HEC online resources. The library provides comfortable spaces for individual and group study【835029197067688†L291-L303】.

UCP offers state‑of‑the‑art laboratories across engineering, IT, pharmacy and science disciplines. These labs provide hands‑on experience and experimental learning to complement classroom instruction【835029197067688†L367-L370】.

A dedicated girls’ hostel on campus provides safe and comfortable accommodation for female students. Facilities include furnished rooms, dining areas and recreational spaces, though detailed amenities are described on the respective accommodation page (not accessible)【835029197067688†L334-L344】.

Ideas Conclave is a university initiative launched in 2020 to create a platform for dialogues between students and leading thinkers, writers, academics and professionals. The series hosts conversations, workshops and roundtables on topics such as intercontinental history, South‑Asian fiction, local government, sustainable cities, constitutionalism, entrepreneurship, climate change and arts. Since its inaugural session in October 2020, Ideas Conclave has organized more than seventeen panel discussions, book launches and interviews with guests from over ten countries【471732857194774†L75-L99】.

Ride with Pride is UCP’s initiative to empower female students by enabling them to commute independently. The program issues scooters equipped with tracking systems and helmets, making it as easy to borrow a two‑wheeler as checking out a library book. It aims to solve commute issues for women and encourages them to participate in daily activities with confidence【471732857194774†L117-L126】.

The Career Services Office (CSO) prepares students and alumni for life beyond university by helping them explore employment opportunities, pursue further education and build connections. Its structure includes three verticals: placements, continuing education and alumni relations. The alumni relations vertical leverages connections to provide mentorship, guidance and placement opportunities and strengthens the alumni network【936966549023621†L75-L87】.

The Registrar Office maintains UCP’s official academic records and ensures adherence to academic policies and standards. Its mission is to foster student progress by maintaining accurate, secure and confidential records while providing exemplary service to students, faculty, staff, alumni and families. Responsibilities include registration services, enrollment and degree verification, class scheduling, grade processing and degree audits. The office values accuracy, clarity, respect for individual dignity and responsiveness【62813686501944†L79-L117】.

UCP’s Quality Enhancement Cell (QEC) is headed by a dean and reports directly to the Vice Chancellor/Rector. QEC promotes public confidence that the quality and standards of the university’s degrees are maintained. It develops quality assurance processes and evaluation methods to ensure academic standards, works with HEC and collects data for analysis. QEC also reviews academic affiliations and supports planning, assessment and improvement of teaching and learning【917258158963171†L60-L98】.

UCP’s Marketing & Communications Department supports the university’s goal of global recognition by providing strategic marketing and media guidance. UCP implements the Higher Education Commission’s policy on protection against sexual harassment in higher education institutions. The policy affirms the right of students and staff to live and work in a safe environment free from sexual harassment, defines prohibited conduct, encourages complaints to be lodged with designated resources, provides options for informal and formal resolution, outlines interim measures such as schedule adjustments and counselling, and establishes a three‑member inquiry committee to investigate and adjudicate complaints

UCP’s ORIC coordinates a diverse portfolio of R&D projects funded by national and international agencies. These projects reflect UCP’s commitment to applied research, industry collaboration and sustainable development

UCP achieved prominent positions in the 2024 UI GreenMetric World University Rankings, placing 4th in Pakistan overall and 2nd among private universities and improving its global ranking from 259 to 206. The university produces 0.5 MW of renewable energy, treats 500 litres of water per day, and operates three reverse‑osmosis plants. UCP actively pursues Sustainable Development Goals, focusing on social equality, health, sustainability and economic growth and aims to reduce inequalities, enhance quality of life and build a sustainable future

Dr. Muhammad Awais Shafique, Associate Professor of Civil Engineering at UCP, participated as a panelist in a climate change and sustainability conference where he discussed green mobility and sustainable transport systems. The UCP Student Handbook outlines the university’s vision to become internationally acclaimed in teaching and research and its mission to provide quality education and prepare youth with skills for research, employment and entrepreneurship. Core values include discipline, integrity, competence, rule of law, dignity and respect. The handbook’s financial support section lists scholarship categories (merit-based, need-based, PGC scholarships, etc.) and continuation CGPA requirements for each, with up to full tuition based on entry marks. The handbook emphasises support services (social support, career services, library, safety and security) and rules on attendance, credit hours, withdrawal, evaluation systems and grades

UCP’s rules and regulations specify admission eligibility (e.g., minimum 45% marks for most undergraduate programmes, 60% for engineering and pharmacy, and 50% for computing; 2.0 CGPA for MS/MPhil and 3.0 CGPA for PhD). They detail credit transfer policies (max 50% credits, minimum grade C+ or B-), registration processes, and refund policies (100% tuition refund in first week, 50% in second week). Attendance rules require at least 75% attendance; students missing more than 25% may be withdrawn. The evaluation system allocates 30–40% weight to assignments and quizzes, 20% to midterm exams and 40–50% to finals, using a relative grading system on a 4‑point scale

UCP’s Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) policy, established during the COVID‑19 period, creates an online academic council, VLE directorate and monitoring committee. The policy mandates that online courses must be supported by the Learning Management System (LMS) and Microsoft Teams, with course descriptions, objectives, resources, lesson plans, grading policies, key dates and progress reports available on the portal. Departments must provide communication tools and ensure compliance with regulatory bodies

The VLE assessment guidelines outline remote assessment strategies: exams may be divided into sections for different student groups; numerical problem parameters should be randomized; students may be required to upload handwritten exams; viva examinations may be conducted after exams; submissions can be accepted via email or WhatsApp in case of technical issues; and adequate time should be provided for students to complete exams

UCP’s VLE portal provides a comprehensive online service for students, teachers and administrators. Student services include grade cards, course registration, timetables, course catalogues, announcements, transcripts, leave status, fee challan printing, feedback, and problem reporting. Teacher services cover term management, student information, course management and outcome‑based education reports. Administrative services include team management, attendance management, course management, user management, grading and registration

UCP maintains numerous partnerships and collaborations with international universities (e.g., Xi’an International Studies University, Arcada University, Umm al‑Qura University, Roslin Institute at the University of Edinburgh, Griffith College), national universities (Islamia University of Bahawalpur, UET, etc.) and corporate organisations (Pakistan Stock Exchange, RCDP, Zong, banks). These collaborations facilitate joint research, exchange programmes, industry seminars and innovative projects

UCP’s scholarship policy for Fall 2025 lists fifteen scholarship categories including PGC students, alumni scholarships, merit-based scholarships, need-based scholarships, scholarships for specially‑abled students, talent-based scholarships, sports-based scholarships, legacy scholarships, scholarships for Gilgit-Baltistan students, educator advancement programmes, scholarships for media and engineering professionals, a post‑graduate fellowship programme and scholarships for students who have lost a parent or guardian. It sets scholarship percentages and continuation CGPA requirements for PGC students by faculty, with categories such as 50% or 25% tuition remission; outlines alumni scholarships (50%, 25%, 12.5%) based on previous CGPA; and defines merit scholarships based on intermediate exam marks (e.g., 100%, 75%, 50%, 25%, 12.5% tuition) with continuation CGPA requirements

Under the Punjab Workers Welfare Fund (PWWF) Scholarship, UCP offers 100% tuition and admission fee waiver to eligible workers’ children. Applicants must apply as regular candidates and then verify eligibility after selection; the worker must have a valid social security card, at least three years of service in a registered factory, and the candidate must provide employment and educational documents. Limited seats are available for LLB and Pharm-D programmes
